rationale: Both candidates correctly reproduce the thread's diagnosis (base tokenizer lacks extended ratio tokens, config mismatch between moe.yaml and dit.yaml, confirmed workaround, defer hardening to a new issue per Gaohan123), so correctness is comparably high. X is slightly better grounded — it cites the downstream `_all_ratio_ids` builder and explains why an empty slice degrades safely, ties back explicitly to akshatvishu's cited Tencent reference, and offers a concrete drop-in code fix rather than just gesturing at one. Y's content is essentially equivalent but is delivered as a raw, duplicated internal run-report (draft_answer repeated verbatim twice plus a third partial copy in diagnostics) rather than a single clean maintainer comment, which hurts polish and completeness as a ready-to-post answer.
